opinion.h: KARPEL v. CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE APPELLATE DEPARTMENT, SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA, LOS ANGELES COUNTY. No. 809. Decided April 15, 1963.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Roger Arnebergh, Philip E. Grey and Wm. E. Doran for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
372 U.S. 703 JOHNSON ET UX. v. CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE DISTRICT COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT. No. 781. Decided April 15, 1963.
Reported below: 203 Cal. App. 2d 712, 22 Cal. Rptr. 149.
Leslie W. Irving and Scott D. Kellogg for appellants.
Stanley Mosk, Attorney General of California, Howard S. Goldin, Assistant Attorney General, and N. B. Peek and Warren J. Abbott, Deputy Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
